name: Lucky Orange Analytics API
what_it_does: Conversion optimization and UX analytics platform with REST API — providing session recordings, heatmaps, form analytics, and live chat in one tool, with API for exporting visitor data, session metadata, and conversion insights.
use_cases: Exporting session recording metadata and visitor segments via API for custom reporting
Querying heatmap data and click events programmatically for UX analysis pipelines
Accessing form analytics data (field abandonment, completion rates) via API
Integrating Lucky Orange visitor data with CRM via data export API
Monitoring recording segments and filtering recordings by custom event criteria
not_for: Product analytics with funnel analysis and retention (use Mixpanel or Amplitude)
Enterprise digital experience intelligence at scale (use Contentsquare or FullStory)
Teams needing real-time API responses for live session control
best_when: A marketing or growth team needs affordable all-in-one UX analytics (heatmaps, recordings, forms, chat) with basic API access for data export.
avoid_when: You need enterprise-scale session analytics, deep product analytics, or real-time programmatic session access.

auth.notes: API key from account settings. Passed as api_key query parameter. Site ID required in requests. Account-level access. No fine-grained scopes. Single API key per account.

pricing.paid_tiers: From $18/month for 5,000 sessions; higher plans available
pricing.notes: Very affordable — starts at $18/month. No credit card for free tier (100 sessions). US-based bootstrapped company. All-in-one alternative to buying separate heatmap, recording, and form analytics tools. API is supplementary to dashboard.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits apply; not publicly documented

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Limited REST API — primarily for visitor data and recording export. API documentation is minimal. No webhooks. JavaScript snippet primary interface. API is supplementary to dashboard usage. Not designed for agent-first workflows.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: All API reads are fully idempotent. No write API exposed — read-only.
known_agent_gotchas: API key in query parameter — logged in server access logs
Very limited API surface — primarily for data export, not programmatic control
API documentation is sparse — may need to reverse-engineer from examples
No webhooks — must poll for new session data

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. GDPR compliant. US data residency. API key in query parameter is a security concern (URL logging). No formal certifications beyond GDPR. Small bootstrapped team — security processes less formal than enterprise vendors.
